title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
After parting ways with an acquaintance at number 61 Albany Road, Colin Lamb walks toward Wilbraham Crescent. He reflects on the death of Mr. Corry (also known as Castleton), noting the lack of witnesses and several unresolved questions: the timing of Corry's anesthesia, the location of the murder, and how his body arrived at number 19. Lamb considers whether interviewing Miss Pemaroo, Miss Waterhouse, or Mrs. Humble might yield useful information, noting that Mrs. Humble’s tangential manner of speaking might contain clues.

Upon arriving at number 19, Lamb finds the street quiet and desolate. He observes that the neighborhood lacks the typical "gossipy" residents who might assist an investigation, as many elderly neighbors have moved to hospitals or nursing homes. While observing a large, modern apartment block, Lamb sees a flash of light from a third-floor window. Using a small bird-watching telescope, he identifies a child with a long braid looking out from the window. 

After an old Rolls-Royce passes by, Lamb approaches the apartment building. Avoiding the empty doorman station, he takes the elevator to the third floor. He locates door number 77 and, deciding that the number seven is lucky, presses the doorbell to see who responds.
